question_html: Write a paragraph describing the causes and effects of corruption in public institutions. 10
solution_html:
The Causes and Effects of Corruption in Public Institutions

Corruption in public institutions arises from a combination of systemic weaknesses and social pressures. At first, inadequate accountability mechanisms allow officials to misuse authority without facing meaningful consequences. Secondly, political influence often interferes with fair recruitment, procurement, and promotions, creating space for favoritism. Thirdly, low wages in certain sectors encourage some employees to seek illegal payments to supplement their income. Moreover, a lack of transparency in administrative procedures makes it easier for bribery and document manipulation to occur. As a result, corruption reduces public trust in government offices and weakens institutional credibility. It also increases the cost of development projects and slows the delivery of public services. Ordinary citizens suffer most when basic services require informal payments to access. Foreign investors may hesitate to invest in countries where corruption is widespread, leading to economic losses. In addition, corruption deepens social inequality by empowering a few while marginalizing the majority. Ultimately, persistent corruption threatens good governance and obstructs long-term national progress.

Marks Distribution:

Highest Marks --------------------------------------------------------------------09 Marks

(A) For Title (The Causes and Effects of Corruption in Public Institutions) ------------------------------------01 Mark

(B) Topic Sentence (Introduction) ---------------------------------------------01 Mark

Corruption in public institutions arises from a combination of systemic weaknesses and social pressures.

C) Context Explanation (The Causes and Effects of Corruption in Public Institutions) ( Any 6 Points) ----06 Marks
‎• Inadequate accountability mechanisms enable officials to misuse authority without consequences.
‎• Political influence affects fair recruitment, procurement, and promotions, fostering favoritism.
‎• Low wages lead some employees to seek illegal payments to augment their income.
‎• Lack of transparency facilitates bribery and document manipulation.
‎• Corruption undermines public trust in government and weakens institutional credibility.
‎• It raises costs for development projects and delays public service delivery, impacting ordinary citizens.
‎• Foreign investors may avoid countries with high corruption, causing economic losses.
‎• Corruption exacerbates social inequality by empowering a few and marginalizing the majority.

D) Conclusion --------------------------------------------------------------------------01 Mark
‎By integrating AI strategically and addressing ethical concerns, Bangladesh can accelerate economic growth, enhance public services, and foster a more inclusive future.

Instructions:

1. For every 3 spelling/grammatical mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted.

2. Students must write within a para. If a student writes more than one para, half of the deserving marks must be deducted. (Comment: Write within single/one para).

3. For writing only the title of the paragraph bears no mark.

4. For writing only the title of the paragraph bears no mark. (comment: Write the Title/ The Causes and Effects of Corruption in Public Institutions)

question_html:
Make meaningful sentences with each of the following words. 5

Instinctively, Feeble, Legitimate, Ruthless, Conservative
solution_html:
Instinctively (Adverb): Birds instinctively know how to build complex nests without any prior learning.

Feeble (Adjective): The patient gave a feeble smile when the doctor assured him of recovery.

Legitimate (Adjective) : Her passport seemed legitimate, but on closer inspection, it was found to have been altered.

Ruthless (Adjective): In business, some people become ruthless when competition threatens their position or success.

Conservative (Adjective) : Older people tend to be more conservative and a bit suspicious of anything new.

Conservative (Noun): As a lifelong conservative, she consistently argued against any increase in government spending and prioritized fiscal responsibility. [Students can use it both as Adjective and Noun]

Instructions:

(i) Any correct and logically meaningful sentence will be accepted. (Sentences with weak meaning will not be allowed.)

(ii) For any grammatical or structural mistake, full mark for this item must be deducted. Examiners must underline and correct the mistake(s) in the scripts.

(iii) For 02 spelling mistakes, 01 mark will be deducted, for 01 spelling mistake, 0.5 marks will be deducted from that very sentence.
‎(iv) For changing the part of speech of the given word by adding a suffix or prefix, the full marks have to be deducted from the very number. But students can use any form if the given word is a verb (depending on the subject-verb agreement, tense, person, number, etc.).
‎Comment: Use the given word without changing its parts of speech.

question_html:
The following is the beginning of a story. Complete it in your own words: 5

Ismail Mian is an established farmer of Dhunchi, a village standing on the bank of the Padma. Though he was born in a poor family, today he is an idol to the hardworking youths of the area. One day, he told them about his success story to……
solution_html:
The Farmer's Legacy

Ismail Mian is an established farmer of Dhunchi, a village standing on the bank of the Padma. Though he was born in a poor family, today he is an idol to the hardworking youths of the area. One day, he told them about his success story to inspire and motivate them. He began by recounting his humble beginnings, when he struggled to make ends meet. Determined to change his fate, Ismail worked tirelessly, experimenting with different crops and farming techniques. He invested in education about modern farming practices and implemented them on his farm.

Through perseverance and innovation, he transformed his small plot of land into a prosperous farm. His methods increased yield and brought him success beyond his dreams. Ismail emphasized the importance of hard work, continuous learning, and adaptability. The youths listened intently, realizing that their dreams were attainable with dedication and effort. Ismail’s journey became a beacon of hope, showing them that with commitment, they too could rise from modest beginnings to achieve greatness.

Marks Distribution

Highest mark---------------05 Marks

(i)Title (Any logical one) -------(01 Mark has to be deducted for not writing the title) “The Farmer's Legacy”

(ii) Story (minimum new 5 sentences) 1×5=05 Marks

{If a student adds or writes at least 5-7 new sentences using the given part and completes the story with a proper description without any grammatical or spelling mistakes, he/she will get full marks}.

Instructions:

(i) If a student doesn’t write the given part, 01 mark has to be deducted (comment: write the given part first).

(ii) Students can write the story in any logical way (sample answer isn’t fixed).

(iii) The given part tells us that the story is the incidents of the Past. So, it is normal that the student will develop the story using Past Tense but it can be described in Present Tense and future Tense also by using direct speech.

(iv) If a student doesn’t write the Title, 01 mark has to be deducted (comment: write the Title/ The

Farmer's Legacy

question_html:
(i) Read the text below and fill in the gaps with the clues given in the box. There are more words than necessary. Make any grammatical changes (if necessary). 5

Education is the process by which our mind (a)_____ through formal learning at an institution like a school, college or university. It is mental and intellectual training which (b)_____opportunities of growth and helps to (c)_____ obstacles to progress. Again the purpose of education is to (d)_____ the individual and develop his/her capacity to limit. It is also the business of education to (e)_____ individuals to make the right choices to go (f)_____. It ennobles our mind and refines our (g)_____. It also (h)_____ our outlook and helps us (i)_____ aware of our rights and (j)____.

(ii) Fill in the gaps with suitable words: 5

Humans can change (a)_______ the sun's (b)_____ nor the earth's orbit around the sun. But they can control the (c)_______ in the amount of greenhouse gases and its effect on the atmosphere. Only during the last hundred years the carbon dioxide (d)_________ has been raised alarmingly in the atmosphere and we humans can be held (e)_________ for this.
solution_html:
(i)

(a) develops

(b) provides

(c) overcome

(d) enlighten

(e) train

(f) ahead

(g) sensibility

(h) broadens

(i) become

(j) responsibilities

Instructions:

Any mistake bears no mark. However, mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.

(ii)

(a) neither

(b) radiation

(c) increase

(d) concentration

(e) responsible

Instructions:

Any mistake bears no mark. However, mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.

question_html:
Translate the following paragraph into English: 5

ধূমপান একটি মারাত্মক খারাপ অভ্যাস। ধূমপানে আসক্ত ব্যক্তি ক্যান্সারে আক্রান্ত হতে পারে। আর ক্যান্সার যে একটি মরণব্যাধি তা বলার অপেক্ষা রাখে না। তাই ধূমপানের বিরুদ্ধে কঠোর অভিযান চালানো এখন বিশেষ প্রয়োজন। ধূমপানের ক্ষতিকর দিক সম্পর্কে বিশদ জ্ঞানসম্পন্ন চিকিৎসাবিদদের এ অভিযানের দিকনির্দেশক হওয়া উচিত।
solution_html:
অনুবাদ: Smoking is a dangerously/deadly bad habit. People addicted to smoking might become victims of cancer. And that cancer is a fatal disease needs no telling. So, a vigorous campaign against smoking is a crying need. Medical professionals/men with their superior knowledge of the harmful effects of smoking should be the leaders of the campaign.

Instructions:
‎* For accurate translation using grammar, punctuation, and sentence structure, students will get full marks. [05 marks for translating 05 sentences correctly]

* For every 03 grammatical/spelling errors 01 mark has to be deducted.

question_html: Explain the proverb: “No pains, no gains.” 5
solution_html:
The proverb ‘No pains, no gains’ signifies the idea that achieving success, improvement, or rewards requires hard work, effort, and perseverance. It suggests that valuable outcomes and accomplishments don't come easily; they demand dedication and sometimes discomfort or sacrifice. Whether in physical endeavours like fitness and sports, academic pursuits, career advancements, or personal growth, the principle says that one must invest time, energy, and often endure challenges to reach significant goals. The proverb acts as a motivational reminder that enduring difficulties and pushing through obstacles are integral parts of the journey toward meaningful and lasting achievements.

Mark Distribution:

A) Main theme of the proverb-----------------01 mark
‎• The proverb “No pains, no gains” emphasizes the idea that achieving success, improvement, or rewards requires hard work, effort, and perseverance.

B) Context of The Proverb (No pains, no gains) (03 points) -----03 marks

• It implies that valuable outcomes require dedication, discomfort, or sacrifice.
‎• Applicable to various fields such as fitness, academics, careers, and personal growth.
‎• Emphasizes the importance of investing time and energy despite challenges.

C) Concluding Sentence……….01 Mark

• Ultimately, the proverb serves as a motivational reminder of enduring difficulties on the path to meaningful achievements.

Instructions:

For every 3 spelling/grammatical mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted.
